cpu-intensive.reason: Lambda has 15-min execution limit and cold starts — poor fit for sustained CPU-bound work

io-bound.reason: All options work; Lambda shines for short-lived I/O

batch.reason: Batch jobs often exceed Lambda duration limits

event-driven.reason: Both handle event-driven well; Lambda is cheaper at low volume

candidateTemplates.label: ECS Fargate + PostgreSQL
candidateTemplates.description: Managed containers with ARM RDS — lowest ops overhead, ARM savings

candidateTemplates.label: EC2 + PostgreSQL + Redis
candidateTemplates.description: Traditional VM with caching — highest control, medium complexity

candidateTemplates.label: Lambda + DynamoDB
candidateTemplates.description: Serverless — zero-server ops, event-driven, variable cost

candidateTemplates.label: ECS Fargate + DynamoDB + Redis
candidateTemplates.description: Containers with NoSQL and caching — high throughput, auto-scale

candidateTemplates.label: EC2 + DynamoDB
candidateTemplates.description: VM with NoSQL — predictable cost, simple ops
